heading: State application
label: 7173 State application
notes: Amendments2015— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section related to State application, consisting of subsecs. (a) to (f).Pub. L. 114–95

Effective Date of 2015 AmendmentAmendment by effective , except with respect to certain noncompetitive programs and competitive programs, see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 114–95section 5 of Pub. L. 114–95section 6301 of this titleDec. 10, 2015

children.text: designates the State educational agency as the agency responsible for the administration and supervision of programs assisted under this part;
describes how the State educational agency will use funds received under this part, including funds reserved for State-level activities;

children.label: I
children.text: schools implementing comprehensive support and improvement activities or targeted support and improvement activities under ; andsection 6311(d) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: other schools determined by the local educational agency to be in need of intervention and support; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the families of such students; and

children.label: B
children.text: will further give priority to eligible entities that propose in the application to serve students described in subclauses (I) and (II) of ;section 7174(i)(1)(A)(i) of this title

describes the procedures and criteria the State educational agency will use for reviewing applications and awarding funds to eligible entities on a competitive basis, which shall include procedures and criteria that take into consideration the likelihood that a proposed community learning center will help participating students meet the challenging State academic standards and any local academic standards;

children.label: A
children.text: of sufficient size and scope to support high-quality, effective programs that are consistent with the purpose of this part; and

children.label: B
children.text: in amounts that are consistent with ;section 7174(h) of this title

describes the steps the State educational agency will take to ensure that programs implement effective strategies, including providing ongoing technical assistance and training, evaluation, dissemination of promising practices, and coordination of professional development for staff in specific content areas and youth development;
describes how programs under this part will be coordinated with programs under this chapter, and other programs as appropriate;

children.label: A
children.text: will make awards for programs for a period of not less than 3 years and not more than 5 years; and

children.label: B
children.text: will require each eligible entity seeking such an award to submit a plan describing how the activities to be funded through the award will continue after funding under this part ends;

contains an assurance that funds appropriated to carry out this part will be used to supplement, and not supplant, other Federal, State, and local public funds expended to provide programs and activities authorized under this part and other similar programs;

contains an assurance that the State educational agency will require eligible entities to describe in their applications under how the transportation needs of participating students will be addressed;section 7174(b) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: prescreen external organizations that could provide assistance in carrying out the activities under this part; and

children.label: B
children.text: develop and make available to eligible entities a list of external organizations that successfully completed the prescreening process;

children.label: A
children.text: an assurance that the application was developed in consultation and coordination with appropriate State officials, including the chief State school officer, and other State agencies administering before and after school (or summer recess) programs and activities, the heads of the State health and mental health agencies or their designees, statewide after-school networks (where applicable) and representatives of teachers, local educational agencies, and community-based organizations; and

children.label: B
children.text: a description of any other representatives of teachers, parents, students, or the business community that the State has selected to assist in the development of the application, if applicable;

describes the results of the State’s needs and resources assessment for before and after school (or summer recess) programs and activities, which shall be based on the results of on-going State evaluation activities;

children.label: i
children.text: are able to track student success and improvement over time;

children.label: ii
children.text: include State assessment results and other indicators of student success and improvement, such as improved attendance during the school day, better classroom grades, regular (or consistent) program attendance, and on-time advancement to the next grade level; and

children.label: iii
children.text: for high school students, may include indicators such as career competencies, successful completion of internships or apprenticeships, or work-based learning opportunities;

children.label: B
children.text: a description of how data collected for the purposes of subparagraph (A) will be collected; and

children.label: C
children.text: public dissemination of the evaluations of programs and activities carried out under this part; and
provides for timely public notice of intent to file an application and an assurance that the application will be available for public review after submission.

children.heading: Deemed approval
children.label: b Deemed approval
children.text: An application submitted by a State educational agency pursuant to subsection (a) shall be deemed to be approved by the Secretary unless the Secretary makes a written determination, prior to the expiration of the 120-day period beginning on the date on which the Secretary received the application, that the application is not in compliance with this part.

children.heading: Disapproval
children.label: c Disapproval
children.text: The Secretary shall not finally disapprove the application, except after giving the State educational agency notice and an opportunity for a hearing.
give the State educational agency notice and an opportunity for a hearing; and

children.label: A
children.text: cite the specific provisions in the application that are not in compliance; and

children.label: B
children.text: request additional information, only as to the noncompliant provisions, needed to make the application compliant.
the expiration of the 45-day period beginning on the date on which the application is resubmitted; or
the expiration of the 120-day period described in subsection (b).

children.heading: Failure to respond
children.label: f Failure to respond
children.text: If the State educational agency does not respond to the Secretary’s notification described in subsection (d)(2) during the 45-day period beginning on the date on which the agency received the notification, such application shall be deemed to be disapproved.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: g Limitation
children.text: The Secretary may not give a priority or a preference for States or eligible entities that seek to use funds made available under this part to extend the regular school day.
